issues.detail: The skill file lacks explicit instructions for the agent, making it vague and ambiguous.
The skill is tailored to Node.js and React, limiting applicability to other platforms.
The skill does not cover all error scenarios or edge cases, such as network failures or authentication errors beyond a simple 403.

scenarios.name: Presence channel with auth
scenarios.rationale: Baseline uses the older pusher.authenticate() method and only binds pusher:subscription_succeeded while omitting member_added/member_removed and the avatar field, whereas the treatment uses authorizeChannel with user_info and binds all three presence lifecycle events.

scenarios.name: Batch trigger to multiple channels
scenarios.rationale: Baseline issues two separate pusher.trigger() calls instead of using pusher.triggerBatch() with an array of {channel, name, data} objects to send both events in a single API call as the treatment does.
